criteria.label: Build a single-command argparse CLI with `--help`, types, and exit codes.
criteria.example: Write `summarize.py` with `--max-tokens INT --format {text,json} FILE`; verify `--help` is informative, bad args exit `2`, success exits `0`.

criteria.label: Expose the CLI as an installed command via `pyproject.toml`.
criteria.example: Add `[project.scripts] ragapp = 'ragapp.cli:main'`; after `pip install -e .`, `which ragapp` returns a real path and `ragapp --help` works.

criteria.label: Design a multi-sub-command CLI with per-command handlers.
criteria.example: Implement `ragapp ingest|index|search` using `add_subparsers()` (or Typer); each sub-command has its own flags and a dedicated handler function.

criteria.label: Respect Unix conventions for I/O and exit codes.
criteria.example: `cat doc.txt | ragapp chunk -` works; results go to stdout; errors go to stderr with code `1`; Ctrl-C exits `130`.

criteria.label: Make the CLI testable in-process.
criteria.example: Tests call `main(['search','q','-k','3'])`, assert return value, and use `capsys` to inspect stdout — no `subprocess.run` needed.

criteria.label: Treat the CLI surface as an API and evolve it carefully.
criteria.example: When renaming `--max-tokens` to `--limit`, add `--limit` as the primary flag, keep `--max-tokens` as a deprecated alias with a stderr warning for two releases, then remove.
